mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS stops rising and inflation growth eases, the discount-rate shock to equities fades and the S&P 500 delivers positive returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL decelerates while FEDFUNDS is already restrictive, because investors then price a nearer peak in policy rates.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change declines below its prior 6-month average
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered observations produce negative 20d SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the average 20d return after triggers is not above the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income and lower earnings expectations, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next quarter. The effect is most visible when UNRATE rises faster than PAYEMS growth, which indicates broad labor deterioration rather than a narrow employment mix shift.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Rejected if 60d forward SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of UNRATE-rise triggers, or if the sign of the average return is non-negative across any 2-year rolling sample of triggered events.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent increase in retail sales indicates stronger consumption demand and better near-term corporate revenue, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should be most reliable when RSAFS is rising on a sequential basis rather than merely bouncing from a depressed level.
failure_condition: Rejected if positive month-over-month RSAFS growth does not coincide with higher-than-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the signal's Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Improving industrial production signals stronger real activity and easing recession odds, which should lift equity returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when INDPRO accelerates after a prior slowdown, because markets reprice cyclical earnings upward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns positive after being negative in the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Rejected if INDPRO acceleration events are followed by negative or zero average 20d SPX returns, or if the hit rate is not above 55% over a 5-year walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts predicts weaker construction activity, slower household demand, and lower cyclical earnings, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. This mechanism is most informative when HOUST remains below its recent trend rather than oscillating around it.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Rejected if repeated HOUST trend breaks are followed by non-negative 60d SPX returns on average, or if the conditional win rate for negative returns falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation running above policy rates erodes real financial conditions and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated while FEDFUNDS has not yet reached a comparable restrictive level.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Rejected if inflation-above-policy triggers are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than half of cases, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate supports consumer incomes and reduces recession risk, which should keep S&P 500 returns positive over the next week when labor conditions remain tight. The effect should be seen when UNRATE is at or below a low-cycle threshold rather than merely trending down from a high level.
failure_condition: Rejected if low-UNRATE episodes fail to outperform baseline 5d returns, or if the conditional positive-return rate is not at least 5 percentage points above the unconditional rate.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP and industrial production both accelerate, the market should infer broad real-activity improvement and bid equities higher over the next quarter. The effect requires both series to point in the same direction; a GDP rebound without industrial recovery is too weak to sustain the signal.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth rises and INDPRO 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Rejected if joint GDPC1-INDPRO acceleration events do not produce positive 60d SPX returns on average, or if the signal loses predictive power and Rank IC falls below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window.
